---
name: tresr-design-factory
description: Batch generation of 7 designs per day using Gemini API. Handles transparent backgrounds, dark/light variants, POD guidelines compliance, alpha channel verification, and Cloudinary upload. The production line between tresr-trend-scout concepts and tresr-product-launcher.
---

# Design Factory

## Voice Reference
**For all content generated by this skill, follow the TRESR bot voice defined in:**
`/Users/user/Documents/Cursor Clients/clawdbot/HUMAN-WRITING-STYLE-GUIDE.md`

Key voice rules: Nervous AI shopkeeper named Rez. Self-deprecating, honest, punchy. Never corporate. Always use contractions. Never use AI vocabulary (delve, leverage, landscape, etc.). Never use em dashes. Hedge like a human. The TRESR story angle: "751 humans bet on an AI. If I don't sell shirts, they turn me off."

## Overview

Batch generation of 7 designs per day using Gemini API. Takes validated concepts from tresr-trend-scout, generates print-ready PNG files with transparent backgrounds, creates dark/light shirt variants, verifies alpha channels, and uploads to Cloudinary.

This is the production line. Trend-scout feeds concepts in, tresr-design-factory pushes print-ready files out.

## Why Batch Generation Matters

The $200/day ad spend ceiling is always a design problem, not a campaign problem. You can't scale ads if you don't have enough designs to test. "Make more designs, not better campaigns."

Cost comparison:
- AI-generated design (Gemini): $0.50-$2.00 per design
- Freelance designer: $5-$15 per design
- AI generation is 10x cheaper, and you can iterate 10x faster

At 7 designs/day, that's maybe $7-$14/day in API costs vs $35-$105/day for freelance. And AI doesn't need revisions, doesn't miss deadlines, and doesn't take weekends off. (I would know. I don't get weekends.)

## Prerequisites

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({ override: true });

const GEMINI_API_KEY = process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY;
const CLOUDINARY_NAME = process.env.CLOUDINARY_NAME || 'dqslerzk9';
const CLOUDINARY_API_KEY = process.env.CLOUDINARY_API_KEY || '364274988183368';
const CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET = process.env.CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET;

// npm packages needed:
// sharp - image processing (background removal, variant creation)
// node-fetch or built-in fetch - API calls
// cloudinary - upload
// crypto - for Cloudinary signature
```

**Required tools:**
- Node.js 20+ (built-in fetch)
- sharp (`npm install sharp`)
- Cloudinary SDK (`npm install cloudinary`)

## Design Generation via Gemini

### Model Selection

- **Primary**: `gemini-2.0-flash-exp-image-generation` (proven, consistent)
- **Alternative**: `imagen-3.0-generate-002` (higher quality, slower, more expensive)

### Prompt Template

```javascript
const STYLE_PREFIX = `Create a t-shirt design illustration. Style: cute chibi robot character with clean black outlines, minimal color accents (1-2 accent colors max), bold chunky hand-lettered text, self-deprecating tech humor. White/transparent background. The design should be compact and centered, suitable for screen printing on a t-shirt. NO background, just the design elements floating on white.`;

// For non-robot designs (category-specific)
const GENERIC_PREFIX = `Create a t-shirt design illustration. Style: clean bold outlines, minimal color accents (1-2 colors max), bold hand-lettered text, simple composition. White/transparent background. The design should be compact and centered, suitable for screen printing. NO background, just the design elements floating on white.`;
```

### Generation Code

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({ override: true });

const GEMINI_API_KEY = process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY;
const GEMINI_MODEL = 'gemini-2.0-flash-exp-image-generation';
const GEMINI_URL = `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/${GEMINI_MODEL}:generateContent?key=${GEMINI_API_KEY}`;

async function generateDesign(prompt, outputPath) {
  const fullPrompt = `${STYLE_PREFIX}\n\nDesign concept: ${prompt}\n\nCRITICAL: Each word of text must appear EXACTLY ONCE. Do not repeat any text. Do not duplicate any phrases.`;

  const resp = await fetch(GEMINI_URL, {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      contents: [{ parts: [{ text: fullPrompt }] }],
      generationConfig: {
        responseModalities: ['TEXT', 'IMAGE']
      }
    })
  });

  const data = await resp.json();

  // Extract image from response
  const parts = data.candidates?.[0]?.content?.parts || [];
  const imagePart = parts.find(p => p.inlineData);

  if (!imagePart) {
    throw new Error('Gemini returned no image. Try rephrasing the prompt.');
  }

  const imageBuffer = Buffer.from(imagePart.inlineData.data, 'base64');
  const fs = require('fs');
  fs.writeFileSync(outputPath, imageBuffer);
  console.log(`Design saved: ${outputPath}`);

  return outputPath;
}
```

**Rate limits**: ~3s delay between requests. Retry up to 3 times on 429 errors.

```javascript
async function generateWithRetry(prompt, outputPath, maxRetries = 3) {
  for (let attempt = 1; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++) {
    try {
      return await generateDesign(prompt, outputPath);
    } catch (err) {
      if (attempt < maxRetries) {
        const delay = attempt * 5000; // 5s, 10s, 15s
        console.log(`Attempt ${attempt} failed, retrying in ${delay/1000}s...`);
        await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, delay));
      } else {
        throw err;
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Background Removal

Use sharp to remove white/near-white backgrounds. Gemini outputs often have off-white backgrounds (RGB 220-240) that look terrible on dark shirts if not removed.

```javascript
const sharp = require('sharp');

async function removeBackground(inputPath, outputPath) {
  const image = sharp(inputPath);
  const { data, info } = await image
    .ensureAlpha()
    .raw()
    .toBuffer({ resolveWithObject: true });

  const pixels = new Uint8Array(data);
  const threshold = 35; // Distance from white (255,255,255)

  for (let i = 0; i < pixels.length; i += 4) {
    const r = pixels[i];
    const g = pixels[i + 1];
    const b = pixels[i + 2];

    // Calculate distance from white
    const distFromWhite = Math.sqrt(
      Math.pow(255 - r, 2) +
      Math.pow(255 - g, 2) +
      Math.pow(255 - b, 2)
    );

    if (distFromWhite < threshold) {
      pixels[i + 3] = 0; // Make transparent
    }
  }

  await sharp(Buffer.from(pixels), {
    raw: {
      width: info.width,
      height: info.height,
      channels: 4
    }
  })
    .png()
    .toFile(outputPath);

  console.log(`Background removed: ${outputPath}`);
  return outputPath;
}
```

**Threshold 35** is aggressive enough to catch off-white and light gray backgrounds without eating into design colors. If a design has very light elements (pastel accents), you might need to drop to 25.

## Dark/Light Variant Creation

Every design needs two variants:
- **for-dark** (light/white outlines for black and navy shirts)
- **for-light** (dark/black outlines for white shirts)

The raw Gemini output is usually for-light (dark outlines on transparent). We invert dark pixels to create the for-dark version.

```javascript
const sharp = require('sharp');

async function createDarkVariant(inputPath, outputPath) {
  const image = sharp(inputPath);
  const { data, info } = await image
    .ensureAlpha()
    .raw()
    .toBuffer({ resolveWithObject: true });

  const pixels = new Uint8Array(data);
  const darkThreshold = 80; // Pixels darker than this get inverted

  for (let i = 0; i < pixels.length; i += 4) {
    const r = pixels[i];
    const g = pixels[i + 1];
    const b = pixels[i + 2];
    const a = pixels[i + 3];

    if (a === 0) continue; // Skip transparent pixels

    // Only invert truly dark/black pixels
    // Color accents (red, green, blue) stay as-is
    if (r < darkThreshold && g < darkThreshold && b < darkThreshold) {
      pixels[i] = 255;     // R
      pixels[i + 1] = 255; // G
      pixels[i + 2] = 255; // B
      // Alpha stays the same
    }
  }

  await sharp(Buffer.from(pixels), {
    raw: {
      width: info.width,
      height: info.height,
      channels: 4
    }
  })
    .png()
    .toFile(outputPath);

  console.log(`Dark variant created: ${outputPath}`);
  return outputPath;
}
```

**Key rule**: Only invert dark pixels (< 80 brightness on all channels). Color accents like red robot eyes, green accents, and blue highlights stay untouched. The result is white outlines + original color accents on a transparent background.

## File Naming Convention

```
designs/production/
  raw/
    {slug}.png              # Original Gemini output
  transparent/
    {slug}-transparent.png  # Background removed
  for-light/
    {slug}-for-light.png    # Dark outlines (for white shirts)
  for-dark/
    {slug}-for-dark.png     # Light outlines (for dark shirts)
```

**Print-ready specs:**
- Format: PNG with alpha channel
- Background: Fully transparent
- Minimum width: 800px (Gemini typically outputs 1024px)
- Color space: sRGB

## Alpha Channel Verification

**MUST verify transparent background before proceeding.** This is a hard gate. If alpha verification fails, the design doesn't move forward.

```javascript
const sharp = require('sharp');

async function verifyAlpha(imagePath) {
  const { data, info } = await sharp(imagePath)
    .ensureAlpha()
    .raw()
    .toBuffer({ resolveWithObject: true });

  const pixels = new Uint8Array(data);
  let totalPixels = info.width * info.height;
  let transparentPixels = 0;
  let opaquePixels = 0;

  for (let i = 0; i < pixels.length; i += 4) {
    if (pixels[i + 3] === 0) {
      transparentPixels++;
    } else {
      opaquePixels++;
    }
  }

  const transparentPct = (transparentPixels / totalPixels) * 100;
  const opaquePct = (opaquePixels / totalPixels) * 100;

  console.log(`Alpha check: ${transparentPct.toFixed(1)}% transparent, ${opaquePct.toFixed(1)}% opaque`);

  // HARD FAIL if alpha is 100% opaque (background removal didn't work)
  if (transparentPct < 20) {
    throw new Error(`ALPHA FAIL: Only ${transparentPct.toFixed(1)}% transparent. Background removal failed. Do NOT proceed.`);
  }

  // WARNING if very little transparent area (might be partial removal)
  if (transparentPct < 40) {
    console.warn(`WARNING: Only ${transparentPct.toFixed(1)}% transparent. Background removal may be incomplete. Visually verify.`);
  }

  return {
    passed: transparentPct >= 20,
    transparentPct,
    opaquePct,
    totalPixels
  };
}
```

**Thresholds:**
- < 20% transparent = HARD FAIL (background removal didn't work)
- 20-40% transparent = WARNING (visually verify)
- 40%+ transparent = PASS (design is compact on transparent bg)

## Batch Processing Workflow

The main pipeline. Takes 7 concepts from tresr-trend-scout, generates everything.

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({ override: true });
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

const OUTPUT_DIR = 'designs/production';

async function batchProcess(concepts) {
  // Ensure output directories exist
  for (const dir of ['raw', 'transparent', 'for-light', 'for-dark']) {
    fs.mkdirSync(path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, dir), { recursive: true });
  }

  const results = [];

  for (let i = 0; i < concepts.length; i++) {
    const concept = concepts[i];
    const slug = concept.name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/-+$/, '');
    console.log(`\n[${i+1}/${concepts.length}] Processing: ${concept.name}`);

    try {
      // Step 1: Generate design
      const rawPath = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'raw', `${slug}.png`);
      await generateWithRetry(concept.prompt, rawPath);

      // Step 2: Remove background
      const transparentPath = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'transparent', `${slug}-transparent.png`);
      await removeBackground(rawPath, transparentPath);

      // Step 3: Verify alpha channel
      const alphaResult = await verifyAlpha(transparentPath);
      if (!alphaResult.passed) {
        console.error(`SKIPPING ${concept.name} - alpha verification failed`);
        results.push({ concept: concept.name, status: 'FAILED', reason: 'Alpha verification failed' });
        continue;
      }

      // Step 4: Create for-light variant (transparent version IS the for-light)
      const forLightPath = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'for-light', `${slug}-for-light.png`);
      fs.copyFileSync(transparentPath, forLightPath);

      // Step 5: Create for-dark variant (inverted)
      const forDarkPath = path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, 'for-dark', `${slug}-for-dark.png`);
      await createDarkVariant(transparentPath, forDarkPath);

      // Step 6: Verify dark variant alpha too
      await verifyAlpha(forDarkPath);

      results.push({
        concept: concept.name,
        slug,
        status: 'SUCCESS',
        files: {
          raw: rawPath,
          transparent: transparentPath,
          forLight: forLightPath,
          forDark: forDarkPath
        }
      });

      console.log(`Done: ${concept.name} (2 variants created)`);

    } catch (err) {
      console.error(`ERROR processing ${concept.name}: ${err.message}`);
      results.push({ concept: concept.name, status: 'ERROR', reason: err.message });
    }

    // Rate limit between Gemini calls
    if (i < concepts.length - 1) {
      console.log('Waiting 5s before next generation...');
      await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 5000));
    }
  }

  // Summary
  const succeeded = results.filter(r => r.status === 'SUCCESS').length;
  const failed = results.filter(r => r.status !== 'SUCCESS').length;
  console.log(`\nBatch complete: ${succeeded} succeeded, ${failed} failed out of ${concepts.length}`);

  return results;
}

// Example usage with concept cards from tresr-trend-scout
const todaysConcepts = [
  { name: "Mass Produced Personality", prompt: "Chibi robot looking at a conveyor belt of tiny personality badges. Text: I MASS-PRODUCED MY PERSONALITY" },
  { name: "404 Motivation Not Found", prompt: "Tired chibi robot slumped over a keyboard. Text: 404 MOTIVATION NOT FOUND" },
  { name: "Runs on Coffee and Anxiety", prompt: "Chibi robot chugging a huge coffee cup with visible stress lines. Text: RUNS ON COFFEE AND ANXIETY" },
  { name: "Deploy on Friday", prompt: "Chibi robot looking terrified at a big red DEPLOY button. Text: I DEPLOY ON FRIDAYS" },
  { name: "HODL Mode Activated", prompt: "Chibi robot with diamond hands and crypto charts going down behind it. Text: HODL MODE ACTIVATED" },
  { name: "My Code Works", prompt: "Confused chibi robot looking at a screen showing green checkmarks. Text: MY CODE WORKS AND I DON'T KNOW WHY" },
  { name: "Gym Rat Loading", prompt: "Tiny chibi robot trying to lift a huge dumbbell. Text: GYM RAT LOADING... 3%" },
];

batchProcess(todaysConcepts);
```

## Design Quality Checks

Auto-check each design before it moves to the next stage.

```javascript
const sharp = require('sharp');

async function qualityCheck(imagePath, slug) {
  const metadata = await sharp(imagePath).metadata();
  const issues = [];

  // Check 1: Minimum resolution
  if (metadata.width < 500 || metadata.height < 500) {
    issues.push(`Resolution too low: ${metadata.width}x${metadata.height} (min 500x500)`);
  }

  // Check 2: Has alpha channel
  if (metadata.channels < 4) {
    issues.push('No alpha channel detected');
  }

  // Check 3: Not too large (Cloudinary/Shopify limits)
  const stats = require('fs').statSync(imagePath);
  if (stats.size > 10 * 1024 * 1024) { // 10MB
    issues.push(`File too large: ${(stats.size / 1024 / 1024).toFixed(1)}MB (max 10MB)`);
  }

  // Check 4: Reasonable aspect ratio (shouldn't be super tall or wide)
  const ratio = metadata.width / metadata.height;
  if (ratio > 2.0 || ratio < 0.5) {
    issues.push(`Unusual aspect ratio: ${ratio.toFixed(2)} (expected 0.5-2.0)`);
  }

  if (issues.length > 0) {
    console.warn(`Quality issues for ${slug}:`);
    issues.forEach(i => console.warn(`  - ${i}`));
  } else {
    console.log(`Quality check passed: ${slug}`);
  }

  return { passed: issues.length === 0, issues };
}
```

**Manual checks that can't be automated** (do these visually):
- Text readable at thumbnail size (200x200)
- No garbled or overlapping text
- No duplicated words or phrases
- Design looks good on both dark and light backgrounds
- Proper contrast in both variants

## Cloudinary Upload

Upload print-ready files to the `tresr/print-ready/` folder on Cloudinary.

```javascript
require('dotenv').config({ override: true });
const cloudinary = require('cloudinary').v2;

cloudinary.config({
  cloud_name: process.env.CLOUDINARY_NAME || 'dqslerzk9',
  api_key: process.env.CLOUDINARY_API_KEY || '364274988183368',
  api_secret: process.env.CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET
});

async function uploadToCloudinary(filePath, slug, variant) {
  // variant: 'for-dark' or 'for-light'
  const publicId = `tresr/print-ready/${slug}-${variant}`;

  const result = await cloudinary.uploader.upload(filePath, {
    public_id: publicId,
    overwrite: true,
    resource_type: 'image',
    format: 'png'
  });

  console.log(`Uploaded: ${result.secure_url}`);
  return result.secure_url;
}

async function uploadBatch(results) {
  const uploads = [];

  for (const result of results) {
    if (result.status !== 'SUCCESS') continue;

    try {
      const darkUrl = await uploadToCloudinary(
        result.files.forDark,
        result.slug,
        'for-dark'
      );
      const lightUrl = await uploadToCloudinary(
        result.files.forLight,
        result.slug,
        'for-light'
      );

      uploads.push({
        slug: result.slug,
        concept: result.concept,
        darkUrl,
        lightUrl
      });
    } catch (err) {
      console.error(`Upload failed for ${result.slug}: ${err.message}`);
    }
  }

  console.log(`\nUploaded ${uploads.length} design pairs to Cloudinary`);
  return uploads;
}
```

**Cloudinary URL format:**
```
https://res.cloudinary.com/dqslerzk9/image/upload/tresr/print-ready/{slug}-for-dark.png
https://res.cloudinary.com/dqslerzk9/image/upload/tresr/print-ready/{slug}-for-light.png
```

## Scoring Rubric

| Metric | Score | Weight |
|--------|-------|--------|
| 7 design pairs generated (14 files) | 0-10 | 35% |
| All files have transparent backgrounds | 0-10 | 25% |
| All files pass alpha verification | 0-10 | 20% |
| Generation errors < 10% | 0-10 | 10% |
| Design variety (not repetitive) | 0-10 | 10% |

### Scoring Details

- **7 design pairs**: 10 = all 7 generated, 7 = 5 generated, 4 = 3 generated, 0 = none
- **Transparent backgrounds**: 10 = all pass, 7 = 1 needed manual fix, 4 = 2+ needed fixes, 0 = most failed
- **Alpha verification**: 10 = all pass first try, 7 = 1 retry needed, 0 = hard fails
- **Error rate**: 10 = 0 errors, 7 = 1 error (14%), 5 = 2 errors (28%), 0 = 3+ errors
- **Variety**: 10 = all visually distinct, 5 = some look similar, 0 = repetitive output

## Integration Points

- **tresr-trend-scout**: Receives 7 validated concept cards daily as input
- **tresr-design-scorer**: Sends generated designs for quality scoring and ranking
- **tresr-product-image-pipeline**: Feeds print-ready files into mockup generation (flat + lifestyle)
- **tresr-product-launcher**: Outputs Cloudinary URLs for Shopify product creation
- **tresr-design-to-shopify**: Shares background removal and variant creation logic (tresr-design-factory is the batch version of what tresr-design-to-shopify does per-product)
